headline: Micron surges 16% on blowout earnings
detail: Micron's monster quarter signals strong AI-driven memory demand, and the positive read-through is lifting chip names broadly. This is directly good for AVGO and SMH in your book — but it may be a short window. Use any pop to trim AVGO (your largest, already flagged) rather than hoping it erases your 13% loss.

headline: Apple drops 6% on MacBook/iPad price hikes from memory crunch
detail: Apple is raising prices because memory costs are spiking, which is a demand headwind for consumer devices. While higher memory prices can benefit chip suppliers short-term, a slowdown in Apple device sales is a broader risk for the tech ecosystem your portfolio is concentrated in. Watch whether this narrative widens beyond Apple.

headline: Bond ETF flows surge as market hunts yield
detail: Heavy rotation into bonds tells you big money is hedging — they're not fully buying this tech rally. For a margin-heavy, tech-concentrated book like yours, that defensive tone in the background is a warning to keep stops tight and not add leverage into strength.
